[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

connectorUiConfig.title: Valimail Enforce Configuration Events
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Valimail Configuration Events](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/13133390-microsoft-sentinel) data connector allows ingesting email domain's configuration events from the Valimail's Reporting API into Microsoft Sentinel. The data connector is built on Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Platform.

dataTypes.name: ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: ValimailEnforceEvents_CL | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

parameters.content:
#### Configuration steps for the Valimail Events API 
 Follow the instructions in the [guide to generate a set of Reporting API credentials](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/11322142-api-key-self-service). Store the created Client ID and the App ID keys.

parameters.description:
The slug will be the string after /app/ in the Valimail's URL once you are logged in. 
 If you are unsure of your account slug, please reach out to support and we will be more than happy to help you find it.
parameters.label: Client Account Slug
parameters.name: accountslug

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend EventTypeNorm = tolower(strcat_delim('_', split(['event-type'], ' '))) | project Subject = subject, User = user, EventType = ['event-type'], EventChange = ['event-change'], PerformedAt = ['performed-at'], TimeGenerated = ['performed-at'], SourceSystem = "Valimail", Computer = user, EventCategory = case(EventTypeNorm contains "user", "UserManagement", EventTypeNorm contains "dmarc", "DMARCPolicy", EventTypeNorm contains "spf", "SPFConfiguration", EventTypeNorm contains "dkim", "DKIMConfiguration", EventTypeNorm contains "domain", "DomainManagement", EventTypeNorm contains "service", "ServiceManagement", "Other"), EventSeverity = case(EventTypeNorm == "dmarc_policy_set_to_none", "High", EventTypeNorm == "user_deleted", "High", EventTypeNorm contains "deleted", "Medium", EventTypeNorm == "dmarc_policy_set_to_quarantine", "Medium", EventTypeNorm == "dmarc_policy_set_to_reject", "Low", EventTypeNorm contains "added", "Low", "Informational"), IsHighValueEvent = EventTypeNorm in ("user_added", "user_deleted", "user_deactivated", "spf_delegation_started", "spf_delegation_stopped", "dkim_key_added", "dkim_key_deleted", "dmarc_policy_set_to_quarantine", "dmarc_policy_set_to_reject", "dmarc_policy_set_to_none")

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: Subject of the performed action. Currently domains and application users

columns.name: User
columns.description: email address or organization that performed the action

columns.name: PerformedAt
columns.description: Date and time (UTC) when the action was performed

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: Date and time (UTC) when the event was registered

columns.name: EventCategory
columns.description: Event category: User, Domain or Service management, DMARC, SPF, DKIM and policy changes

columns.name: EventSeverity
columns.description: Impact of the change: high, medium,low or informational

columns.name: IsHighValueEvent
columns.description: True if the event can impact email deliverability

connectorUiConfig.title: Valimail Enforce Configuration Events
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Valimail Configuration Events](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/13133390-microsoft-sentinel) data connector allows ingesting email domain's configuration events from the Valimail's Reporting API into Microsoft Sentinel. The data connector is built on Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Platform.

dataTypes.name: ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: ValimailEnforceEvents_CL | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

parameters.content:
#### Configuration steps for the Valimail Events API 
 Follow the instructions in the [guide to generate a set of Reporting API credentials](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/11322142-api-key-self-service). Store the created Client ID and the App ID keys.

parameters.description:
The slug will be the string after /app/ in the Valimail's URL once you are logged in. 
 If you are unsure of your account slug, please reach out to support and we will be more than happy to help you find it.
parameters.label: Client Account Slug
parameters.name: accountslug

properties.description:
Detects deletion of SPF delegations or DKIM keys, which are medium-severity events
that could degrade email authentication posture for a domain.
properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Email Authentication Key Deleted
properties.query:
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventSeverity == "Medium"
| where EventCategory in ("SPFConfiguration", "DKIMConfiguration")
| where IsHighValueEvent == true
| summarize
    EventCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen = max(PerformedAt),
    AffectedDomains = make_set(Subject),
    Actions = make_set(EventType)
  by User, EventCategory
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain = tostring(split(User, "@")[1]),
    DomainName = tostring(AffectedDomains[0])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat:
User '{{User}}' deleted one or more {{EventCategory}} records affecting
domains: {{AffectedDomains}}. This may weaken email authentication posture.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Email Authentication Key Deleted

properties.description:
Detects when a DMARC policy is changed to 'none', which disables enforcement
and leaves the domain vulnerable to spoofing and phishing attacks.
properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Weakened to None
properties.query:
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventSeverity == "High"
| where EventType == "dmarc_policy_set_to_none"
| summarize
    EventCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen = max(PerformedAt),
    Changes = make_set(EventChange)
  by Subject, User, EventCategory
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain = tostring(split(User, "@")[1]),
    DomainName = Subject

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: DMARC policy set to NONE on domain {{Subject}} by {{User}}
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat:
The DMARC policy for domain '{{Subject}}' was set to 'none' by '{{User}}',
disabling email authentication enforcement. This may expose the domain to spoofing.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Weakened to None

properties.description:
Detects when a single user performs more than 3 configuration changes or user
additions within a 1-hour window on any domain. An unusual burst of changes may
indicate a compromised admin account, unauthorized automation, or insider threat.
properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Unusual Rate of Configuration Changes or User Additions
properties.query:
let threshold = 3;
let timeWindow = 1h;
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventCategory in (
    "DMARCPolicy",
    "SPFConfiguration",
    "DKIMConfiguration",
    "DomainManagement",
    "UserManagement"
  )
| summarize
    ChangeCount       = count(),
    HighValueCount    = countif(IsHighValueEvent == true),
    Domains           = make_set(Subject),
    DomainCount       = dcount(Subject),
    Actions           = make_set(EventType),
    Categories        = make_set(EventCategory),
    FirstSeen         = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen          = max(PerformedAt)
  by User, bin(PerformedAt, timeWindow)
| where ChangeCount > threshold
| extend
    AccountName   = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain = tostring(split(User, "@")[1]),
    DomainName    = tostring(Domains[0]),
    ChangesPerMin = round(todouble(ChangeCount) / 60.0, 2)

alertDetailsOverride.alertDisplayNameFormat: Unusual change rate by {{User}} — {{ChangeCount}} changes in 1h across {{DomainCount}} domain(s)
alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat:
User '{{User}}' made {{ChangeCount}} configuration changes ({{HighValueCount}} high-value)
across {{DomainCount}} domain(s) within one hour. Categories affected: {{Categories}}.
Actions: {{Actions}}.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Unusual Rate of Configuration Changes or User Additions

properties.description:
Detects high-severity user management events such as user deletion or deactivation
in the Valimail Enforce platform, which may indicate unauthorized access or insider threat.
properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - High-Value User Management Event
properties.query:
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventCategory == "UserManagement"
| where EventSeverity == "High"
| where IsHighValueEvent == true
| summarize
    EventCount = count(),
    FirstSeen = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen = max(PerformedAt),
    AffectedUsers = make_set(Subject),
    Actions = make_set(EventType)
  by User, EventCategory
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain = tostring(split(User, "@")[1])

alertDetailsOverride.alertDescriptionFormat:
User '{{User}}' performed {{EventCount}} high-value user management
action(s) in Valimail Enforce. Actions: {{Actions}}

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - High-Value User Management Event
Valimail Enforce - Bulk Domain Changes by Single User
properties.query:
let threshold = 5; // flag users who touched more than this many domains
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where IsHighValueEvent == true
| summarize
    DomainCount = dcount(Subject),
    Domains = make_set(Subject),
    Actions = make_set(EventType),
    FirstSeen = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen = max(PerformedAt)
  by User, bin(PerformedAt, 1h)
| where DomainCount > threshold
| extend
    AccountName = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain = tostring(split(User, "@")[1])
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, User, DomainCount, Domains, Actions
| order by DomainCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Hunt for users who have made configuration changes to an unusually high number
of domains in a short period. May indicate a compromised admin account or
unauthorized bulk reconfiguration.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Bulk Domain Changes by Single User
Valimail Enforce - Configuration Change Rate Trend
properties.query:
let threshold = 3;
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventCategory in (
    "DMARCPolicy",
    "SPFConfiguration",
    "DKIMConfiguration",
    "DomainManagement",
    "UserManagement"
  )
| summarize
    ChangeCount    = count(),
    HighValueCount = countif(IsHighValueEvent == true),
    Actions        = make_set(EventType),
    Categories     = make_set(EventCategory),
    Domains        = make_set(Subject),
    DomainCount    = dcount(Subject)
  by User, bin(PerformedAt, 1h)
| extend
    ExceedsThreshold = ChangeCount > threshold,
    ChangesPerMin    = round(todouble(ChangeCount) / 60.0, 2),
    AccountName      = tostring(split(User, "@")[0]),
    AccountDomain    = tostring(split(User, "@")[1])
| project
    PerformedAt,
    User,
    ChangeCount,
    HighValueCount,
    DomainCount,
    ChangesPerMin,
    ExceedsThreshold,
    Domains,
    Actions,
    Categories
| order by PerformedAt desc

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Hunt for configuration change and user addition activity grouped by user and
domain over hourly buckets. Use this to establish baselines, spot unusual
spikes, and investigate specific users or domains flagged by the
corresponding analytics rule.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - Configuration Change Rate Trend
Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Change History
properties.query:
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where EventCategory == "DMARCPolicy"
| project
    PerformedAt,
    Domain = Subject,
    ChangedBy = User,
    PolicyAction = EventType,
    ChangeDetail = EventChange,
    Severity = EventSeverity,
    IsHighValue = IsHighValueEvent
| order by PerformedAt desc

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Hunt for all DMARC policy changes across domains over the selected time range.
Helps identify domains that have had their enforcement posture changed and by whom.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - DMARC Policy Change History
Valimail Enforce - High Value Event Summary
properties.query:
ValimailEnforceEvents_CL
| where IsHighValueEvent == true
| summarize
    EventCount = count(),
    Domains = make_set(Subject),
    Actions = make_set(EventType),
    FirstSeen = min(PerformedAt),
    LastSeen = max(PerformedAt)
  by User, EventCategory, EventSeverity
| order by EventCount desc

tags.name: description
tags.value:
Summarizes all high-value Valimail Enforce events over the selected time range,
grouped by category and user. Good for periodic security reviews and baselining
normal admin activity.

properties.displayName: Valimail Enforce - High Value Event Summary

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Valimail API solution provides ability to bring the Valimail Configuration events to your Microsoft Sentinel Workspace to inform and to examine potential security risks, analyze your team's use of collaboration, diagnose configuration problems and more.

Data Connectors: 1, Analytic Rules: 4, Hunting Queries: 4

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
